1в†’#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2в†’################################################################################
     3в†’#Input:
     4в†’# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
     5в†’#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
     6в†’# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
     7в†’#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
     8в†’#       be appended to the default compilation flags
     9в†’#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
    10в†’#       defined in this script will be applied.
    11в†’# $3: Specified compiler
    12в†’#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
    13в†’#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
    14в†’#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
    15в†’#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
    16в†’# $4: Library output path
    17в†’#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
    18в†’#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
    19в†’#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
    20в†’#
    21в†’# Example:
    22в†’#   ./build.sh D10
    23в†’# Or
    24в†’#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
    25в†’# Or
    26в†’#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
    27в†’# Or
    28в†’#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
    29в†’###############################################################################
    30в†’
    31в†’#----- build switches -----
    32в†’BUILD_LIBNN=1
    33в†’BUILD_DOC=0
    34в†’MAKE_INSTALL=1
    35в†’
    36в†’# common compilation flags
    37в†’CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"
    38в†’
    39в†’#convert CPU name to lower case
    40в†’CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)
    41в†’
    42в†’#set the default compilation flags
    43в†’if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    44в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    45в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    46в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    47в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    48в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    49в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    50в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    51в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    52в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    53в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    54в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    55в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    56в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    57в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    58в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    59в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    60в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    61в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    62в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    63в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    64в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    65в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    66в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    67в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    68в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    69в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    70в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    71в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    72в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    73в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    74в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    75в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    76в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    77в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    78в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    79в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    80в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    81в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    82в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    83в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    84в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    85в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    86в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    87в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    88в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    89в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    90в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    91в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    92в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    93в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    94в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    95в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    96в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    97в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    98в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    99в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   100в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
   101в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
   102в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
   103в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
   104в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
   105в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   106в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
   107в†’    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
   108в†’        CC_TMP="${3}"
   109в†’    else
   110в†’        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
   111в†’    fi
   112в†’
   113в†’    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
   114в†’    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
   115в†’    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
   116в†’        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
   117в†’    else
   118в†’        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
   119в†’    fi
   120в†’
   121в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   122в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
   123в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
   124в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   125в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   126в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
   127в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
   128в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   129в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
   130в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   131в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   132в†’elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
   133в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
   134в†’    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
   135в†’else
   136в†’    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
   137в†’    >&2 echo
   138в†’    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
   139в†’    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
   140в†’    exit 1
   141в†’fi
   142в†’
   143в†’# compiler
   144в†’if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
   145в†’    CC="${3}"
   146в†’else
   147в†’    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
   148в†’fi
   149в†’
   150в†’# customized optimization for 45-series
   151в†’if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
   152в†’    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
   153в†’fi
   154в†’
   155в†’# extra compilation flags
   156в†’if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
   157в†’    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
   158в†’fi
   159в†’
   160в†’# output path
   161в†’LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
   162в†’if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
   163в†’    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
   164в†’else
   165в†’    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
   166в†’fi
   167в†’
   168в†’#==================== extra optional flags ====================
   169в†’# -----
   170в†’# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
   171в†’# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
   172в†’# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
   173в†’# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
   174в†’# -----
   175в†’# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
   176в†’${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
   177в†’if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   178в†’    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
   179в†’    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
   180в†’    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
   181в†’    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   182в†’        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
   183в†’        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
   184в†’            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
   185в†’        fi
   186в†’    fi
   187в†’fi
   188в†’
   189в†’INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
   190в†’GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
   191в†’RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"
   192в†’
   193в†’# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
   194в†’cd ${LIB_ROOT}
   195в†’nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
   196в†’if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
   197в†’    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
   198в†’fi
   199в†’
   200в†’if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
   201в†’    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly
   202в†’    nprocessors=$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
   203в†’    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
   204в†’    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then
   205в†’        # for the case of total processor number is less than 16
   206в†’        nprocessors=1;
   207в†’    fi
   208в†’
   209в†’    if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
   210в†’        cd ${LIB_ROOT}
   211в†’
   212в†’        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
   213в†’        AR_FLAG="crD"
   214в†’        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
   215в†’        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
   216в†’        libhvm_name="libhvm"
   217в†’        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
   218в†’        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"
   219в†’
   220в†’        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
   221в†’        echo "**********************************************"
   222в†’        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
   223в†’        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
   224в†’        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   225в†’        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}
   226в†’
   227в†’        # disable the HVM bank awared
   228в†’        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h
   229в†’
   230в†’        # build the libhvm
   231в†’        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
   232в†’        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   233в†’        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   234в†’        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
   235в†’        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
   236в†’        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
   237в†’        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
   238в†’        echo "**********************************************"
   239в†’
   240в†’        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
   241в†’    fi
   242в†’
   243в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   244в†’    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
   245в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   246в†’    echo
   247в†’    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   248в†’    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   249в†’    ret=$?
   250в†’    echo
   251в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   252в†’    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
   253в†’        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
   254в†’    else
   255в†’        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
   256в†’    fi
   257в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   258в†’    echo
   259в†’fi
   260в†’
   261в†’#Document generation
   262в†’if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   263в†’    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
   264в†’    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
   265в†’    rm -rf ./docs
   266в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   267в†’    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
   268в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   269в†’    echo
   270в†’    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1
   271в†’    mv log_make_latex.txt ${LIB_ROOT}
   272в†’    cd ..
   273в†’    mkdir -p pdf
   274в†’    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf
   275в†’    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
   276в†’    cd ${CUR_DIR}
   277в†’    echo
   278в†’    echo "Done!"
   279в†’    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
   280в†’    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"
   281в†’    echo "PDF file: Docs/docs/pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf"
   282в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   283в†’fi  #BUILD_DOC
   284в†’
   285в†’#Installation
   286в†’if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
   287в†’    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   288в†’    rm -rf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}/*
   289в†’    cp -f ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   290в†’    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   291в†’
   292в†’    if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   293в†’        cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs/docs/pdf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   294в†’    fi
   295в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   296в†’    echo " Andes NN Library installation done!"
   297в†’    echo " install folder: ${RELEASE_FOLDER}"
   298в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   299в†’    echo
   300в†’fi  #MAKE_INSTALL
   301в†’
   302в†’exit $ret
   303в†’

1в†’#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2в†’################################################################################
     3в†’#
     4в†’# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
     5в†’# performance.
     6в†’#
     7в†’# Input:
     8в†’#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
     9в†’#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
    10в†’#       of libdsp.
    11в†’#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12в†’#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13в†’#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14в†’#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15в†’#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16в†’#       environment variable before using this script.
    17в†’#   $3: Simulator name
    18в†’#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19в†’#   $4: Simulation target
    20в†’#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21в†’#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22в†’#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23в†’#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24в†’#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25в†’#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26в†’#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27в†’#              will be verified in this mode.
    28в†’#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29в†’#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30в†’#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31в†’#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32в†’#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33в†’#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34в†’#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35в†’#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36в†’#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37в†’#       used to build the library.
    38в†’#   $7: Specified linker
    39в†’#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40в†’#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41в†’#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42в†’#
    43в†’# Example:
    44в†’#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45в†’#
    46в†’#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
    47в†’#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
    48в†’#
    49в†’#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50в†’#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
    51в†’#
    52в†’#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
    53в†’#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
    54в†’#
    55в†’#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
    56в†’#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    57в†’#
    58в†’#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
    59в†’#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    60в†’#
    61в†’# Note:
    62в†’#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
    63в†’#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
    64в†’#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
    65в†’#   be used in the accuray test later.
    66в†’#

    72в†’function check_coverage {
    73в†’    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    74в†’    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    75в†’    cd ${GCOV_DIR}
    76в†’
    77в†’    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    78в†’    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
    79в†’        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
    80в†’        IFS=-
    81в†’        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
    82в†’        IFS=
    83в†’        coverage_status=""
    84в†’        is_coverage_fail=0
    85в†’
    86в†’        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
    87в†’        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
    88в†’            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
    89в†’                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
    90в†’                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
    91в†’                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
    92в†’                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
    93в†’                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
    94в†’                    # coverage_status+=""
    95в†’                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
    96в†’                        is_coverage_fail=1
    97в†’                    fi
    98в†’                fi
    99в†’
   100в†’                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")
   101в†’                break
   102в†’            fi
   103в†’        done
   104в†’
   105в†’        for called_fun in ${called_fun_list[@]}; do
   106в†’            fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
   107в†’            # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
   108в†’            if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
   109в†’                fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
   110в†’                coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
   111в†’                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
   112в†’                    is_coverage_fail=1
   113в†’                fi
   114в†’            fi
   115в†’        done
   116в†’
   117в†’        if [ ${is_coverage_fail} -eq 1 ]; then
   118в†’            coverage_fail_list+=("${coverage_status}")
   119в†’            echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST}
   120в†’            cp $(basename ${gcda/gcda/c.gcov}) ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
   121в†’        fi
   122в†’
   123в†’        # write coverage results into file
   124в†’        echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COV_CSV}
   125в†’    done
   126в†’
   127в†’    # go back to previous path
   128в†’    cd ${CURRENT_PATH}
   129в†’}

   135в†’calculate_code_size () {

   139в†’    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"

   141в†’    #use the linker specified by the user
   142в†’    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
   143в†’        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
   144в†’    fi
   145в†’
   146в†’    # build the executable
   147в†’    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   148в†’
   149в†’    # get the size info
   150в†’    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
   151в†’    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
   152в†’    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}
   153в†’
   154в†’    # calculate the size of each section
   155в†’    awk '

  1128в†’# Build the library with empty functions for measuring the code size. Remember
  1129в†’# to build this library before building the non-empty version library, or it
  1130в†’# will influence the code coveratge checking results.
  1131в†’##################################################
  1132в†’if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
  1133в†’    build_lib "${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_EMPTY_FUN"
  1134в†’
  1135в†’    # move the empty version library to the temporary folder to avoid being
  1136в†’    # removed later as bulding the non-empty version library
  1137в†’    mv "${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/libnn.a" "${EMPTY_LIB}"
  1138в†’fi
  1139в†’
  1140в†’##################################################
  1141в†’# Build the non-empty library.
  1142в†’##################################################
  1143в†’if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == DAILY ]]; then
  1144в†’    build_lib "${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
  1145в†’fi
  1146в†’
  1147в†’##################################################
  1148в†’# start to execute the unit tests
  1149в†’##################################################
  1150в†’if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then
  1151в†’    log
  1152в†’    log "****************************************************"
  1153в†’    log "Begin the accuracy and performance testing ..."
  1154в†’    log
  1155в†’
  1156в†’    #---- enable the configuration diagnosis program for ELF toolcahins -----
  1157в†’    if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] && [[ ! ${CC} == *linux* ]]; then
  1158в†’        target_cfg_diagnosis
  1159в†’    fi
  1160в†’
  1161в†’    #TODO: the inst/cycle field needs to be mofiled (they may not show just one time)
  1162в†’    if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
  1163в†’        #init csv title
  1164в†’        if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} == 1 ]; then
  1165в†’            echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${PERF_CSV}
  1166в†’        fi
  1167в†’    fi
  1168в†’
  1169в†’    if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
  1170в†’        echo "group, function, parameters" > ${PARAM_CSV}
  1171в†’    fi
  1172в†’
  1173в†’    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
  1174в†’        # init the csv title
  1175в†’        echo "fun_name,text,code,rodata,data,bss" > ${SIZE_CSV}
  1176в†’    fi
  1177в†’
  1178в†’    #----- enable the LM switch -----
  1179в†’    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
  1180в†’        sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
  1181в†’    fi
  1182в†’
  1183в†’    for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
  1184в†’        fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
  1185в†’        fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
  1186в†’
  1187в†’        if [[ ${CC} == *linux* ]] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == BOARD ]]; then
  1188в†’            # As using the linux toolchain and the test target is board, this
  1189в†’            # script is only used to generate the executables.
  1190в†’            build_executable "${fun_name}"
  1191в†’
  1192в†’            # copy the required binary files for the unit tests
  1193в†’            cp -r ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/bin ${TEST_LOG_DIR}
  1194в†’
  1195в†’            # copy the running executables for glibc on board script
  1196в†’            cp ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/run_executable_for_glibc.sh ${TEST_LOG_DIR}
  1197в†’
  1198в†’            # enable the write permission for others
  1199в†’            chmod o+w ${TEST_LOG_DIR}
  1200в†’        else
  1201в†’            run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
  1202в†’        fi
  1203в†’    done
  1204в†’
  1205в†’    #----- restore the LM switch -----
  1206в†’    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
  1207в†’        sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
  1208в†’    fi
  1209в†’
  1210в†’    if [ ${CODE_COVERAGE} == "1" ]; then
  1211в†’        if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
  1212в†’            run_special_case_for_rvv
  1213в†’        fi
  1214в†’
  1215в†’        mkdir -p ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
  1216в†’        check_coverage
  1217в†’
  1218в†’        if [ ${#coverage_fail_list[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  1219в†’            rm -rf ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
  1220в†’        fi
  1221в†’    fi
  1222в†’
  1223в†’    #clean gcov output files
  1224в†’    rm -rf ${GCOV_DIR}
  1225в†’    rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.gc{da,no}
  1226в†’    rm -f ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gc{da,no};
  1227в†’
  1228в†’    log
  1229в†’    log "End of accuracy and performance test."
  1230в†’    log "****************************************************"
  1231в†’fi
  1232в†’
  1233в†’##################################################
  1234в†’# show accuracy and coverage results only when building library successfully
  1235в†’##################################################
  1236в†’if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then
  1237в†’    #show result summary
  1238в†’    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
  1239в†’    executed_num=$((total_num - accuracy_unsupport_num))
  1240в†’    log
  1241в†’    log "========== summary =========="
  1242в†’    log "Total functions:       ${#test_list[@]}"
  1243в†’    log "Executed functions:    ${executed_num}"
  1244в†’    if [[ ${executed_num} -eq 0 ]]; then
  1245в†’        log "Passed functions:      ${accuracy_pass_num} (pass rate: 0.00%)"
  1246в†’    else
  1247в†’        log "Passed functions:      ${accuracy_pass_num} (pass rate: $(echo ${accuracy_pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l | xargs printf "%.2f")%)"
  1248в†’    fi
  1249в†’    log "Unsupported functions: ${accuracy_unsupport_num}"
  1250в†’    log
  1251в†’
  1252в†’    # show accuracy fail list
  1253в†’    if [ ${#accuracy_fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
  1254в†’        log
  1255в†’        log "--- failed funcion list: ---"
  1256в†’        for FUN_NAME in "${accuracy_fail_list[@]}"; do
  1257в†’            log ${FUN_NAME}
  1258в†’            echo ${FUN_NAME} >> ${ACCURACY_FAIL_LIST}
  1259в†’        done
  1260в†’        log
  1261в†’    fi
  1262в†’
  1263в†’    # show coverage fail list
  1264в†’    if [ ${#coverage_fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
  1265в†’        log
  1266в†’        log "--- coverage failed function list: ---"
  1267в†’        for FUN_AND_COV in "${coverage_fail_list[@]}"; do
  1268в†’            log ${FUN_AND_COV}
  1269в†’        done
  1270в†’        log
  1271в†’    fi
  1272в†’fi
  1273в†’
  1274в†’#generate error log (diff_log)
  1275в†’FINAL_RESULT=$(( ${build_lib_result} + ${#accuracy_fail_list[@]} ))
  1276в†’if [ ${FINAL_RESULT} -ne 0 ]; then
  1277в†’    echo "${FINAL_RESULT}" > ${ERROR_LOG}
  1278в†’    cp -f ${ERROR_LOG} ${TEST_LOG_DIR}/${ERROR_LOG}
  1279в†’    cp -f ${ERROR_LOG} ../${ERROR_LOG}
  1280в†’fi
  1281в†’
  1282в†’log "***************************************************************************************"
  1283в†’log "FINAL_RESULT=${FINAL_RESULT}"
  1284в†’log End at `date`
  1285в†’FINISH_TIME=`date +%s`
  1286в†’log Total time is $((FINISH_TIME-BEGIN_TIME)) seconds.
  1287в†’log All log file are saved at ${TEST_LOG_DIR} folder.
  1288в†’log "    Full path=${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}"
  1289в†’log libnn accuracy testing end.

     7в†’#----- build and run switches -----
     8в†’ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
     9в†’ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0

    44в†’
    45в†’
    46в†’#----- app running configuration -----

    48в†’# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
    49в†’# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling

    55в†’elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then

    57в†’elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then

   140в†’    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then

   144в†’
   145в†’if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then

   156в†’        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log

   159в†’
   160в†’if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
   161в†’    echo

   170в†’    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then

1в†’#!/bin/bash
     2в†’#set -x
     3в†’
     4в†’OLD_PWD=`pwd`
     5в†’ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
     6в†’THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"
     7в†’
     8в†’# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
     9в†’# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
    10в†’#
    11в†’# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
    12в†’# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
    13в†’# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.
    14в†’
    15в†’# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
    16в†’SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}
    17в†’
    18в†’# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
    19в†’if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    20в†’    echo
    21в†’    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    22в†’    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    23в†’    echo
    24в†’fi
    25в†’
    26в†’# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
    27в†’if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    28в†’    echo
    29в†’    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    30в†’    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    31в†’    echo
    32в†’    exit 1
    33в†’fi
    34в†’
    35в†’# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
    36в†’RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
    37в†’
    38в†’# check if the execution file is available
    39в†’if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    40в†’    echo ""
    41в†’    echo "No such file $1"
    42в†’    echo ""
    43в†’    exit 1
    44в†’fi
    45в†’
    46в†’# get the executable file
    47в†’RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH
    48в†’
    49в†’# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
    50в†’GCC="no"
    51в†’
    52в†’# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
    53в†’CACHE="on"
    54в†’
    55в†’# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
    56в†’RESET_BOARD="on"
    57в†’
    58в†’# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
    59в†’if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    60в†’    RESET="on"
    61в†’    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
    62в†’else
    63в†’    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
    64в†’fi
    65в†’
    66в†’# physically invoke simulator
    67в†’if test -r CVALSTDIN
    68в†’then
    69в†’	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET CVALSTDIN
    70в†’else
    71в†’	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET
    72в†’fi
    73в†’# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
    74в†’# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
    75в†’ret=$?
    76в†’
    77в†’cd ${OLD_PWD}
    78в†’
    79в†’exit ${ret}
    80в†’

# Set some variables

# Setup the timeout value while simulating each test case on real board.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set timeout 500"
set timeout 1200

# Setup the nds32 gdb full-path so that we can execute it to connect to aice.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.

#Setup the port opened by iceman connecting to real board.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.

set port $::env(MYPORT)

send "set pagination off\n"

set elf [lindex $argv 0]
set gcc [lindex $argv 1]
set cache [lindex $argv 2]
set reset [lindex $argv 3]
set stdin [lindex $argv 4]

  spawn $gdb -e "$elf"
} else {

}

	set status 123
	send "quit\n"
	expect {

			send "y\n"
		}
	}

### 1. TOOLCHAIN SPECIFICATION

#### A. **Via build.sh Script**

The `build.sh` script determines the toolchain based on CPU selection (parameter $1):

**Key Mechanism**: CPU-to-Toolchain Mapping
- **NDS32 (V3)**: nds32le-elf-

- **RV32 (32-bit RISC-V)**: riscv32-elf-
  - N225, D23, N25, D25, A25, A27, N45, D45, A45, A46MP в†’ `riscv32-elf-gcc`
  
- **RV64 (64-bit RISC-V)**: riscv64-elf-
  - NX25, AX25, AX27, NX45, AX45, AX65, NX27V, AX46MP, AX46MPV в†’ `riscv64-elf-gcc`
  
- **x86/x64 (Host)**: Empty prefix
  - `gcc` (host compiler, no prefix)

**File Location**: `/local/nick/libnn/build.sh` (lines 40-141)

**Usage Examples**:
```bash
./build.sh D25                                    # Default: riscv32-elf-gcc
./build.sh D25 "-funroll-loops"                   # Add extra flags
./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc               # Custom compiler (parameter $3)
./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang               # Alternative compiler (clang)
```

#### B. **Compiler Selection Logic**

1. **Default compiler** (parameter $3 empty):
   ```bash
   CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
   # e.g., "riscv32-elf-gcc", "riscv64-elf-gcc"
   ```

2. **Custom compiler** (parameter $3 specified):
   ```bash
   CC="${3}"
   # e.g., "riscv32-elf-clang", "riscv64-linux-gcc"
   ```

#### C. **Compile Flags by CPU**

Different CPUs enable different extensions via `-m` flags:

| CPU | Key Flags |
|-----|-----------|
| D10, D15, D23, D25, A25, A27, A45, A46MP | `-mext-dsp` (DSP extension) |
| N225, D23 | `-mext-zc` (compressed) |
| NX27V, AX45MPV, AX46MPV, AX66 | `-mext-vector` + RVV version macros |
| nx27v/ax45mpv | `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000` (RVV 1.0) |
| nx25v_freya | `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80` (RVV 0.8) |
| 45-series | `-DENA_45_SERIES_OPT` (auto-added) |

**File Location**: `/local/nick/libnn/build.sh` (lines 43-131)

---

### 2. QEMU CONFIGURATION

#### A. **QEMU Wrapper Scripts**

QEMU is invoked through **per-CPU wrapper scripts** in project directories.

**Pattern**: `/local/nick/libnn/project/<CPU>/riscv-qemu-wrapper-<variant>`

**Examples**:
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx25/riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25`
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx27v/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_s512_b512` (v-ext variant)
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/ax46mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`

#### B. **QEMU Wrapper Configuration Structure**

Each wrapper script:
1. **Sets QEMU binary** from environment variable:
   ```bash
   QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}     # or QEMU_SYS_RV32
   ```

2. **Specifies CPU model**:
   ```bash
   CPU=andes-nx25                                    # Simple CPU
   CPU=andes-nx27v,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512          # Vector CPU with parameters
   ```

3. **Selects config file** (dynamic for v-ext variants):
   ```bash
   CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/ADP-AE350-NX25.cfg       # Simple config
   # For v-ext, selects from qemu_cfg/<VEC_CFG>/ directory
   CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
   ```

4. **Runs QEMU with flags**:
   ```bash
   $QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU \
         -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF}
   ```

#### C. **Vector Extension Configuration (VEC_CFG)**

For v-ext CPUs, vector configuration is selected via environment variable `VEC_CFG`:

```bash
# Supported values:
# zve32x, zve32f, zve64x, zve64f, zve64d (default if not set)

```


QEMU config files specify CPU ISA features and memory configuration.

#### E. **QEMU Run Mode**

#### A. **test.sh Target Selection (Parameter $4)**

```bash
../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <TARGET> <BS3/DAILY> <flags>
```

Valid targets:
- **GDB**: NDS32 GDB simulator (V3/nds32 only)
- **SID**: Andes System Integration Design simulator (for legacy hardware)
- **QEMU**: QEMU system emulator (modern, RISC-V and V-ext support)
- **BOARD**: Physical hardware via ICEman/GDB expect scripts
- **ALIOTH**: Andes-specific board variant

**Examples**:
```bash
../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3     # GDB simulator
```

#### A. **Board Simulator Wrappers**

### 1. TOOLCHAIN SPECIFICATION

#### A. **Via build.sh Script**

The `build.sh` script determines the toolchain based on CPU selection (parameter $1):

**Key Mechanism**: CPU-to-Toolchain Mapping
- **NDS32 (V3)**: nds32le-elf-
  - N10, D10, D15 в†’ `nds32le-elf-gcc`
  
- **RV32 (32-bit RISC-V)**: riscv32-elf-
  - N225, D23, N25, D25, A25, A27, N45, D45, A45, A46MP в†’ `riscv32-elf-gcc`
  
- **RV64 (64-bit RISC-V)**: riscv64-elf-
  - NX25, AX25, AX27, NX45, AX45, AX65, NX27V, AX46MP, AX46MPV в†’ `riscv64-elf-gcc`
  
- **x86/x64 (Host)**: Empty prefix
  - `gcc` (host compiler, no prefix)

**File Location**: `/local/nick/libnn/build.sh` (lines 40-141)

**Usage Examples**:
```bash
./build.sh D25                                    # Default: riscv32-elf-gcc
./build.sh D25 "-funroll-loops"                   # Add extra flags
./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc               # Custom compiler (parameter $3)
./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang               # Alternative compiler (clang)
```

#### B. **Compiler Selection Logic**

1. **Default compiler** (parameter $3 empty):
   ```bash
   CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
   # e.g., "riscv32-elf-gcc", "riscv64-elf-gcc"
   ```

2. **Custom compiler** (parameter $3 specified):
   ```bash
   CC="${3}"
   # e.g., "riscv32-elf-clang", "riscv64-linux-gcc"
   ```

#### C. **Compile Flags by CPU**

Different CPUs enable different extensions via `-m` flags:

| CPU | Key Flags |
|-----|-----------|
| D10, D15, D23, D25, A25, A27, A45, A46MP | `-mext-dsp` (DSP extension) |
| N225, D23 | `-mext-zc` (compressed) |
| NX27V, AX45MPV, AX46MPV, AX66 | `-mext-vector` + RVV version macros |
| nx27v/ax45mpv | `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000` (RVV 1.0) |
| nx25v_freya | `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80` (RVV 0.8) |
| 45-series | `-DENA_45_SERIES_OPT` (auto-added) |

**File Location**: `/local/nick/libnn/build.sh` (lines 43-131)

---

### 2. QEMU CONFIGURATION

#### A. **QEMU Wrapper Scripts**

QEMU is invoked through **per-CPU wrapper scripts** in project directories.

**Pattern**: `/local/nick/libnn/project/<CPU>/riscv-qemu-wrapper-<variant>`

**Examples**:
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx25/riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25`
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx27v/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_s512_b512` (v-ext variant)
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/ax46mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`

#### B. **QEMU Wrapper Configuration Structure**

Each wrapper script:
1. **Sets QEMU binary** from environment variable:
   ```bash
   QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}     # or QEMU_SYS_RV32
   ```

2. **Specifies CPU model**:
   ```bash
   CPU=andes-nx25                                    # Simple CPU
   CPU=andes-nx27v,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512          # Vector CPU with parameters
   ```

3. **Selects config file** (dynamic for v-ext variants):

   ```bash
   # For v-ext, selects from qemu_cfg/<VEC_CFG>/ directory
   CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
   ```

4. **Runs QEMU with flags**:
   ```bash
   $QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU \

   ```

#### C. **Vector Extension Configuration (VEC_CFG)**

For v-ext CPUs, vector configuration is selected via environment variable `VEC_CFG`:


```ini
property = "vlen"
value = "512"

value = "64"
```

Here's a summary of how toolchain, QEMU, and FPGA/board info are specified:

## Toolchain

Specified via `build.sh` and `test.sh` parameters:

```bash
./build.sh <CPU>                          # Auto-selects toolchain prefix by CPU
./build.sh <CPU> "" riscv32-linux-gcc     # Override compiler via $3
```

CPU в†’ prefix mapping:
- N10/D10/D15 в†’ `nds32le-elf-`
- N225/D23/D25/A27/... в†’ `riscv32-elf-`
- NX25/AX25/NX27V/... в†’ `riscv64-elf-`
- x86/x64 в†’ host `gcc`

## QEMU

Selected via **test.sh target** (`$4 = QEMU`) and a **wrapper script** (`$3`):

```bash
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25 QEMU BS3
```

- Wrapper scripts live in `project/<CPU>/riscv-qemu-wrapper-*`
- They read env vars `QEMU_SYS_RV64` / `QEMU_SYS_RV32` for the QEMU binary path
- For v-ext CPUs (nx27v, ax45mpv, etc.), multiple wrapper variants exist for different VLEN configs (v128, v256, v512)
- Vector sub-extension selected via `VEC_CFG` env var (`zve32x`, `zve32f`, `zve64x`, `zve64f`, `zve64d` вЂ” default is `zve64d`)
- QEMU `.cfg` files in `project/<CPU>/qemu_cfg/<VEC_CFG>/` define CPU ISA features, VLEN, ELEN, cache config

## FPGA / Board

Selected via **test.sh target** (`$4 = BOARD`) with board wrapper scripts:

```bash
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3
```

Key environment variables:
| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `MYPC` | Host running ICEman (board debugger) |
| `MYPORT` | GDB-ICEman socket port |

Board wrappers in `project/<CPU>/` use GDB expect scripts (`gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`) to connect to the physical board via `target remote $MYPC:$MYPORT`, load, and run.

Custom linker scripts (e.g., `project/d25/ae350-xip.ld`) are applied automatically for BOARD targets. Local memory mode is enabled with `-DENA_LM` in extra flags.

   181в†’    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then

   183в†’        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
   184в†’            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
   185в†’        fi
   186в†’    fi
   187в†’fi
   188в†’
   189в†’INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
   190в†’GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
   191в†’RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"
   192в†’
   193в†’# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
   194в†’cd ${LIB_ROOT}

   196в†’if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
   197в†’    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
   198в†’fi

   200в†’if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
   201в†’    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly

   203в†’    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
   204в†’    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then

   207в†’    fi

   211в†’

   213в†’        AR_FLAG="crD"
   214в†’        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
   215в†’        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
   216в†’        libhvm_name="libhvm"
   217в†’        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
   218в†’        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"
   219в†’
   220в†’        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
   221в†’        echo "**********************************************"
   222в†’        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
   223в†’        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
   224в†’        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   225в†’        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}
   226в†’
   227в†’        # disable the HVM bank awared
   228в†’        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h
   229в†’
   230в†’        # build the libhvm
   231в†’        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
   232в†’        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   233в†’        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   234в†’        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
   235в†’        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
   236в†’        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
   237в†’        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
   238в†’        echo "**********************************************"
   239в†’
   240в†’        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
   241в†’    fi
   242в†’
   243в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   244в†’    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
   245в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   246в†’    echo
   247в†’    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   248в†’    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   249в†’    ret=$?
   250в†’    echo
   251в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   252в†’    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
   253в†’        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
   254в†’    else
   255в†’        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
   256в†’    fi
   257в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   258в†’    echo
   259в†’fi
   260в†’
   261в†’#Document generation
   262в†’if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   263в†’    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
   264в†’    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
   265в†’    rm -rf ./docs
   266в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   267в†’    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
   268в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   269в†’    echo
   270в†’    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1

   272в†’    cd ..

   275в†’    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
   276в†’    cd ${CUR_DIR}
   277в†’    echo
   278в†’    echo "Done!"
   279в†’    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
   280в†’    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"

   282в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   283в†’fi  #BUILD_DOC
   284в†’
   285в†’#Installation
   286в†’if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
   287в†’    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}

   290в†’    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   291в†’

   298в†’    echo "**********************************************"
   299в†’    echo

   301в†’
   302в†’exit $ret

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#Input:
# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
#       be appended to the default compilation flags
#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
#       defined in this script will be applied.
# $3: Specified compiler
#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
# $4: Library output path
#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
#
# Example:
#   ./build.sh D10
# Or
#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
# Or
#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
###############################################################################

#----- build switches -----
BUILD_LIBNN=1
BUILD_DOC=0
MAKE_INSTALL=1

# common compilation flags
CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#convert CPU name to lower case
CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)

#set the default compilation flags
if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
        CC_TMP="${3}"
    else
        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
    fi

    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
else
    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
    >&2 echo
    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
    exit 1
fi

# compiler
if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
    CC="${3}"
else
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
fi

# customized optimization for 45-series
if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
fi

# extra compilation flags
if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
fi

# output path
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
else
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
fi

#==================== extra optional flags ====================
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
# -----
# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"

# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
cd ${LIB_ROOT}
nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
fi

if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly
    nprocessors=$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then
        # for the case of total processor number is less than 16
        nprocessors=1;
    fi

    if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
        cd ${LIB_ROOT}

        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
        AR_FLAG="crD"
        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
        libhvm_name="libhvm"
        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"

        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
        echo "**********************************************"
        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}

        # disable the HVM bank awared
        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h

        # build the libhvm
        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
        echo "**********************************************"

        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
    fi

    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
    ret=$?
    echo
    echo "**********************************************"
    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
    else
        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
    fi
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
fi

#Document generation
if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
    rm -rf ./docs
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1
    mv log_make_latex.txt ${LIB_ROOT}
    cd ..
    mkdir -p pdf
    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf
    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
    cd ${CUR_DIR}
    echo
    echo "Done!"
    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"
    echo "PDF file: Docs/docs/pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf"
    echo "**********************************************"
fi  #BUILD_DOC

#Installation
if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    rm -rf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}/*
    cp -f ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}

    if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
        cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs/docs/pdf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
    fi
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Andes NN Library installation done!"
    echo " install folder: ${RELEASE_FOLDER}"
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
fi  #MAKE_INSTALL

exit $ret

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
#
#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
#
#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
#
#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
#
# Note:
#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
#   be used in the accuray test later.
#
################################################################################

#######################################################
# check the coverage of executed functions
#######################################################
function check_coverage {
    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    cd ${GCOV_DIR}

    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
        IFS=-
        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
        IFS=
        coverage_status=""
        is_coverage_fail=0

        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
                    # coverage_status+=""
                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
                        is_coverage_fail=1
                    fi
                fi

                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")

     2в†’
     3в†’#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
     4в†’RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
     5в†’                    #  - 0: standalone mode
     6в†’                    #  - 1: GDB server mode

    23в†’    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    24в†’    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    25в†’    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
    26в†’else

    28в†’fi

    35в†’    exit 1
    36в†’fi
    37в†’

    39в†’RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

    41в†’# Run qemu
    42в†’EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
    43в†’if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    44в†’    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
    45в†’fi
    46в†’$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

    49в†’cd ${OLD_PWD}
    50в†’exit ${ret}

#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-nx27v,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

#!/bin/bash
#set -x

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
#
# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    echo
fi

# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    echo
    exit 1
fi

# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
GCC="no"

# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
CACHE="on"

# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
RESET_BOARD="on"

# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    RESET="on"
    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
else
    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
fi

# physically invoke simulator
if test -r CVALSTDIN
then
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET CVALSTDIN
else
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET
fi
# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

#Path info
CURRENT_PATH=$(pwd)                                 #position of user invokes this script
LIB_ROOT_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)         #position of libnn repository root folder
WRAPPTER_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CURRENT_PATH}/..)     #the wrapper position of BS3
UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Examples/unit_func"
#export each cpu project here to make sure the sim wrapper could be found in daily test ???
# export PATH=$( find ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/ -type d -printf ":%p" ):$PATH

#Output files/folders
TIME_STR=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
TEST_LOG="test_sh_${TIME_STR}.log"      #save the ouput message of this script.
TEST_LOG_DIR="test_sh_${TIME_STR}"
PERF_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/performance.csv"
PARAM_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/parameter.csv"
LOG_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/log"
ADX_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/adx"           # collect the executables into TEST_LOG_DIR
OBJDUMP_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/objdump"   # collect the disassembly into TEST_LOG_DIR
GCOV_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/gcov"         # temporary folder to keep gcov outputs
FAILED_GCOV_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/faild_gcov"
ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
ACCURACY_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/accuracy_fail_list"
COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/coverage_fail_list"
COV_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/coverage.csv"  # keep all functions' code coverage
# COV_FAIL_LIST="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/coverage_fail_list"
LIB_OBJ_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/lib_objs"                 # all functions' object file
TMP_LIB_DIR="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/lib_dir"                  # temporary folder to keep the empty library
EMPTY_LIB="${TMP_LIB_DIR}/${LIB_NAME}_empty.a"
SIZE_CSV="${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/size.csv"     # keep all functions' code size

#Save input arguments into meaningful variables
CC="${2}"                   #compiler name
SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"          #simulator name
TEST_TARGET="${4}"          #GDB / SID / BOARD
TEST_TYPE="${5}"            #BS3 / DAILY
EXTRA_FLAG="${6}"           #extra flags for selecting multi-lib
LD="${7}"                   #specified linker

# setup the binutil full name
SIZE="${CC%-*}-size"
GCOV="${CC%-*}-gcov"
OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"

#Compilation flags and related info
DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="-O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"

#default CPU option
if [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="d10"
    else
        CPU_NAME="n10"
    fi
elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv32"* ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="a45"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="a25"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="n45"
    else
        CPU_NAME="n25"
    fi
elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv64"* ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="ax45"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-dsp"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="ax25"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=100"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=100"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="nx27v"
    elif [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mtune=andes-45-series"* ]]; then
        CPU_NAME="nx45"
    else
        CPU_NAME="nx25"
    fi
fi

# add the mtune option for 25-series
if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na](x?)25$ ]] || [[ ${CPU_NAME} == "d25" ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -mtune=andes-25-series"
fi

# enable ENA_MEASURE_PERF, ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV and ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV when called from test_perf.sh
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} == *"-DENA_TEST_PERF"* ]]; then
    ENA_MEASURE_PERF=1
    ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=1
    ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV=1
fi

#test sat-ext when using V3 toolcahin and without enabling -mext-dsp
#(sat-ext will be tested after plain C)
# if [[ ${CC} == "nds32"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-dsp"* ]];
#     SAT_LIB_CFLAGS="DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS -DENA_EXT_SAT_INST"
#     TEST_SAT_EXT="1"
# else
#     TEST_SAT_EXT="0"
# fi

#check code coverage only on daily test
#note. the code coverage testing is not supported on board
if [ "${TEST_TYPE}" == "DAILY" ] && [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(SID|QEMU)$ ]] && [[ "${CC}" != *"clang" ]] && [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 0 ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} --coverage -DGCOV"
    CODE_COVERAGE="1"
else
    CODE_COVERAGE="0"
fi

DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -Wl,-gc-sections"
if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    # only enable the performance counter for elf toolcahins
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS+=" -DPF_COUNTER"

    # enable virtual hosting / semihosting
    if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} =~ ^(BOARD|QEMU|ALIOTH)$ ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mvh"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == "BOARD" ]] && [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_QILAI_LD"* ]]; then
        # applying the customized linker script for Qilai platform
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx27v_qilai/qilai.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "nds32"* ]]; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V3)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d10/nds32.ld"
    elif [[ "${CC}" == "riscv"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} != *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "ENA_LM" ]] ; then
        # move rodata to LM with the customized linker script (for V5)
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/d25/ae350-xip.ld"
    fi
fi

# app and lib cflags for v-ext
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FS_VS"
    fi

    # As using v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    if [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
        # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
        # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    fi
fi

# extra cflags for the environment with HVM
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-DENA_HVM"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -mcmodel=large"
fi

# setup the extra flags for the glibc toolchain
if [[ ${CC} =~ "linux" ]] && [[ ! ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "-static" ]]; then
    DEFAULT_LIB_CFLAGS+=" -fpic"
fi

# customized crt0 for v-ext 0.8
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} = *"NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"* ]]; then
    DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -nostartfiles ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx25v/crt0.s"
fi

#header files and their search path
if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == BS3 ]]; then
    # As verifying the unit tests with BS3, we should use the header files is
    # the used toolchain instead of those in libnn repo.
    TOOLCHAIN_PATH=$(which ${CC})
    if [[ ${CC} == *elf* ]]; then
        # for elf toolchain
        PREFIX=${CC%-*}
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../${PREFIX}/include")
    else
        # for linux toolchain
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../sysroot/usr/include")
    fi
    INC_DIR="-I${INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN} -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
else
    # As verifying the unit tests during developing, we should use the header
    # files in libnn repo; otherwise, the prototypes of new functions can't
    # be found by the compiler.
    INC_DIR="-I${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
fi

#Some identification flags
if [[ ${CC} == "nds32be"* ]]; then
    ENDIAN="be"
else
    ENDIAN="le"
fi

if [[ ${CC} == *"linux"* ]]; then
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="1"
else
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="0"
fi

#select the library to be linked (from built-in or manual building library)
